entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
Hi @Gopinath,

 

Could you please explain how it works after the Onboardee hire process is complete?

 

Will the Employee Type change from "Onboardee" to "Employee" in IAS automatically on their First day? 

 

Another question I have is, if we have a IAS tenant integrated with SF before 2022, do we have to upgrade to SCIM IPS Connector as of now? Despite the recommendation, is it mandatory? Or only if we want to integrate Onboardees with IAS?

 

Thank you in advance!

 

Best Regards,

Mafalda

entry.title: Re: JDBC Receiver: 'com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServe...
entry.content: Hi, Thank you for your suggestion. I just wanted to ask—could this issue be related to the Cloud Connector or the Database? Based on my observations, the issue doesn’t seem to be related to a specific record. For example, one day we processed 101 jobs/messages from ECC, and 22 failed. On another day, with the same data, 85 jobs failed with the error "Connection reset." Since the failures are inconsistent, I believe the issue might not be data-related. Please share your thoughts on this.

entry.title: SAC Automatic Datalocks workflows
entry.content:
Hello, 

i'm trying to make a dat alock run automatically but is still asking for permission but I don't know what we are doing wrong. 

The person that is owner of the data lock task is also assignee and is the owner of the locks inside of the model but the task is asking for permission to run. Why is this sitll happening? Here are some pictures: 

Datalock cancel because it haven't been approved 

Owner of the datalock in the dimension geography and in the dimmension audit trail. "Diego Moratilla"

Owner and asignee of the task.

Thank you so much for your help

Stephan

entry.title: SAP S4 HANA GROUP REPORTING BEST PRACTIcES

I am new to SAP Group reporting , i want to explore the content of GR Best practices 

i found the below in the learning journey but i can't find the active links , thanks

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model – Cron expression for Process Scheduler
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

This blogpost for the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud – Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM) will introduce the enhancement of our Process Scheduler. As you have read in the last blogpost of 2024 Recurring Process and Multi-Selection of Activities in Process Scheduler, we have introduced the improvements of the Process Scheduler with regards to scheduling a job in recurring scenarios and being able to select multiple activities.

We are happy to announce the latest improvements in the Process Scheduler which we are adding the cron format of scheduling a job to accommodate our customers' needs . To support this feature, we have added a new field called Schedule Type, where users can select between One Time, Repeat Interval and Cron type for job scheduling. Let’s take a look at these different schedule types below:

One Time
This schedule type is basically almost the original state of the Process Scheduler when it was initially introduced in this blogpost Process Scheduler: Execution made easy by Scheduling a “Job” in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud which allows the user to configure a one-time job. Moreover, you are now capable of selecting multiple activities inside the process template/process instance that will run sequentially as introduced in the recent enhancement. Here’s how to create a job using One Time schedule type.

After maintaining the fields and choosing CONFIRM button, the created job will be visible in the job list panel with “Scheduled” state.

Once the planned launch date and time is reached, the state of the job will be in “Completed” status while the Run Status and the Result in the job details panel will be populated.

Repeat Interval
This schedule type was the enhancement we have mentioned in this blogpost Recurring Process and Multi-Selection of Activities in Process Scheduler - we can schedule a job in recurring scenario using Human-Readable Date Formats. Here's what it looks like:

In this job example, we are scheduling a recurring job of every 5 minutes from 16:25 to 16:35 which will give us a total of 3 runs. After selecting the CONFIRM button, the job will be created with the "Scheduled" status and the "Repeat Interval" column will be populated with the schedule parameter we maintained in the "Add Process Schedule" dialogue. After the "Active Until" date and time was reached, the state of the job will be in "Completed" state and all the runs will be updated with run status and results. 

Spoiler
Note: If the "Active Until" field is not maintained, the job will run indefinitely or until the job has been deleted in the Process Scheduler screen.

Cron
This schedule type was introduced to accommodate scheduling of jobs recurrently in a more complex way. You may refer to this documentation to know more about using Cron Format.

The Cron schedule type will introduce the following new fields:

  1. XS Cron Expression - This is a free text field to which the users can input their cron expressions (In the example above, it means the job will run every hour at 12:00 to 09:00 UTC between Mondays to Fridays).
  2. Active Since - Specify the date and time when the scheduled job will start running. This field is not mandatory. If left blank, the first recurrence will be schedule at the earliest possible time after the job creation according to the maintained cron expression. 
  3. Active Until - Specify the date and time when the scheduled job will stop running. This field is also not mandatory. If left blank, the job will be running indefinitely, or until deleted.
Spoiler
IMPORTANT! Cron uses the Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). If you are to use Cron as schedule type, the time that you have to enter in the cron expression field should be in UTC. 

If you want to use Cron but do not know how to write one, we got you covered! You can use the "Generate Basic XS Cron Expression" picker beside the XS Cron Expression field . This option is introduced to help our beginner users to use this schedule type. Bear in mind that the picker cannot do all the combinations that technical users can produce manually. Since it is a free text field, after you have generated a cron expression using the picker, you can still edit the cron expression according to your requirements. You can pick from Daily, Weekly, Monthly and Yearly options. Let's talk about it one by one. 

Daily
If you choose Daily as frequency, it will run everyday at a given time. As shown below, I'm choosing to run the job daily at 08:00. 

Since my system time is in UTC+08:00, when I hit CONFIRM, the generated Cron expression will be scheduled at 12:00 UTC. 

Weekly
If you choose weekly as frequency, you can run a job in a specific time and day/days of the week. For my example below, I will run the job at 18:00 every Monday, Wednesday and Friday. Please see below:

The generated cron expression will look like this. 

Again, it does not look like I chose 18:00 as the maintained time but 10:00 since the cron generator will automatically convert the maintained system time to UTC time. 

Monthly
If we choose monthly as frequency, you can run a job in a specific time in specific day/days of the month. In my example below, I want to run a job at 18:00 every 1st, 10th, 15th, 20th and 30th day of the month. 

The generated cron expression will look like this:

Yearly
If you choose yearly as frequency, you can choose the specific time, months and days of the month accordingly. In my example below, I want to run my job on the 1st, 15th and 30th of January, June and December at 18:00. 

The generated cron will look like this:

After selecting the CONFIRM button, the state of the job will be in "Scheduled" state and the XS Cron Expression column header will show the maintained cron expression.

And there you have it. Like and share this blog post with your colleagues who may be interested in this new feature. This information is also available in our Application Help. 

Thank you!

entry.title: Reminder for Import Certificate
entry.content:
Hello All,

Due to the new tariffs imposed by the US on EU countries, every time a European sales organization creates a sales order for materials listed and where the recipient is a sales organization in the US, a pop-up (reminder) about attaching the import certificate should appear.

I have a list of approximately 500 materials for which, when creating a sales order with a recipient plant in the US, a message must appear indicating the necessity to attach the import certificate.

Do you have any suggestions which solution may I use for that?

Thank you in advance for your support.
BR

entry.title: Re: Is there a solution to prevent personal email on from...
entry.content:
Hi,

the Email in the Spotlight is the Email Address stored in Basic Info.

The Email address in Basic Info gets filled via HRIS Sync hardcoded with the Email address within the Contact Info for Email Address Type B (=Business). If the Personal Email stored in the Contact Info, that Email Address is not synchronized to the Basic Info and not shown in the spotlight. 

Please give more details where you see the personal email in the Spotlight.

 

BR,
Sandra

entry.title: Re: How to cancel Store order ID with Learning Hub subscr...
entry.content:
Hi @Manidipa 
Please follow-up the ticket. The assigned agent has updated it.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Delete Business Partner in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud i...
entry.content:
Hello Jerry,

thank you for your quick reply.

We do not think that created transactional data is a problem, as this was already queried and finalised by us when the work agreement was deleted. We were also able to delete business partners who had created postings in the system.

I try to find CDS View for BP Relationship but can not find for example I_BPRELATIONSHIP in our system. Can you name any other CDS View?

Best Regards

Tim

entry.title: Re: Depreciation area 01 does not manage values for revaluation
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: workflow for Purchase order value change
entry.content:
Hello,

we need to change the workflow for Purchase order change. The order value difference should be indicator of new release instead of the new order value in general.

The BAdi SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF can be used to archive this. WF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL is the to-be-used BAdi-defintion.

I have been investigating how to add a new field in the BADI that covers the value changes in the PO, setting specific value from which the workflow is triggered.
It seems only those fields available in the CDS view are available as no custom fields/structures can be added inside of the BADI. Value change is not in this view, so I do not find a way to add this condition in the BADI. Has someone used value change with this BADI? I think it may not be possible.

An alternative is to use Restart Purchase Order Workflow using SSCUI 103345 and BAdI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART. https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3053155

Customizing needs to be changed in transaction SIMG to make value change as "conditional restart".

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: convert a fixed term employed employee to a permanent...
entry.content:
Hi,

when you have hired an employee via "Add new employee for Fixed Term" this employee has already a termination record. You might need to rehire the employee after the termination date.

Regarding the contract End Date field: The control of the visibility is done via Business Rule onChange of the Contract type. You might need to check that business rule, if the permanent related contract type is listed there to set the visibility to hide for the contract end date field.

 

BR,

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Early Warning System Set up (EWS) in SAP MM/WM
entry.content: That is yet another great feature sadly gone from S/4HANA.

entry.title: Re: Depreciation area 01 does not manage values for reval...
entry.content:
Hi Misaki,

 

If this error messages occurs the underlying AssetValuationView settings do not allow the value type of revaluation. Thus, the desired transaction cannot be posted in this local GAAP. If you consider this to be an error, please create a support ticket for the country version where you experience the issue and ask for clarification. They will check and decide on the next steps where Expert Configuration will not be an option.

Best regards,

 Mick

entry.content:
Dear Team,

We want to implement SSO fr our customer on RISE, with multiple systems - SAP GUI, FIORI Apps, SF, C4C etc.

What's the best method. Please suggest.

Regards,

Debadata

What if we want Directly the URL on PO approval screen in My inbox

SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Enable Now error upload of potentially dangerous cont...
entry.content: Go to Tools > Settings > Producer > General, and deselect the Forbid Active Content checkbox.

entry.title: Re: Translation Solution
entry.content: Google Translate will let you cut/paste...

entry.title: Re: MF50-Planning table basic step
entry.content: Is there any possible way to add the Hours in Period view of MF50. In the Period there are only Days, Weeks, Months and Shifts are available.

entry.title: Re: READ method not executing in RAP Web API V4
entry.content:
that's normal.

eml read will hit your read method.

odata query is hitting the cds if I am not wrong.

entry.title: Re: What is the purpose of recognition and re-recognition...
entry.content:
That sounds like an exam question.

To ensure that SAP Enable Now can accurately and consistently identify elements on the screen. (So if the Author assigns a Hotspot (or Control in a Simulation) to a specific element on the screen, that same exact element can be found during playback.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration: How to use Password Credentials for Outbound Request
entry.content:
Hi Carlos,

I am using the option3 to connect to a 3rd party application which works only with grant_type as password.

However on testing the connection in the destination window, I get the following message.

While in the IFlow, I get the error as 

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Access Token was not found in the response, cause: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Access Token was not found in the response

Can you help what could be an issue?

entry.title: Re: Intermittent Login Issues with Trainer
entry.content: Likely to be a response time issue - the browser is timing out the request before it has received a response from the server. Try increasing the response time limits in Manager, and/or the browser timeout limits in the Registry (there's a SAP Note on this - 2390539).

entry.title: Track record changes done via ImpEx
entry.content:
If someone logs in to HAC, changes something via Impex do we have trace of this who did the changes like we have in BO as last changes?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Track record changes done via ImpEx
entry.content: Changes done to the records via ImpEx can't be tracked in Backoffice using "Last Changes" list. However such changes can be tracked using Generic Audit if that particular type was enabled for auditing.

entry.title: Re: SEN on prem fails create project with "now write perm...
entry.content: Wouldn't the 'no write permission' error be triggered if someone else has it checked out for editing? (IDK, on-prem isn't my strong suit...).

entry.title: Re: I have been given the Admin role in SEN Manager, but...
entry.content: You also need permissions on the individual Role. Go to Administration > User Roles, select the Role and then click the Manage Access button.

entry.title: Re: One-time vendor PO
entry.content: Do you know where the One time Vendor address is provided in the PO template. I think it's in the Header_address worksheet, but not absolutely sure, and can not find any help on this, so far.

entry.title: Re: Email Notifications in SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation
entry.content:
@moritzgysler,

How the Regional settings/Code Page/Unicode could be managed for automatic notification.

Currently, in the newly CALM tenant we receive notifications via standard service with not properly displayed Cyrillic (see attached)

Will appreciate any advice!

Asya

entry.title: Re: I have been given the Admin role in SEN Manager, but...
entry.content: For the Reports, you now also need the specific View: Reports with personal data permission added to one of the roles you have assigned to you.

entry.title: Re: Enable Now error upload of potentially dangerous cont...
entry.content: Thank you so much! I deselected in the settings.

entry.title: Hello, I have a dump in transaction MFBF
entry.content:
Dump in transaction MFBF when I confirm quantity  

PERFORM_NOT_FOUND      CX_SY_DYN_CALL_ILLEGAL_FORM  SAPLV01F 

 

This is because of the non creation of subroutine CHMVS_600 in program saplv01f

The line of the code is   

PERFORM (distform) IN PROGRAM saplv01f. 

But same foms exist CHMVS_001, CHMVS_002, CHMVS_003

Do you know how avoid this error ?

But why the system check it as it does not exist

 

I create a new procedure in "Define batch determination Quantity Proposal Procedure" 600 in custo 

 

Extract standard code source

 

IF NOT gv_receipt IS INITIAL.  
 IF xkondh-chmvs >= 600. 
           distform-prenam = k_prenam.
          distform-suffix = xkondh-chmvs.
          PERFORM (distform) IN PROGRAM saplv01f.
ELSE. "If xkondh-chmvs >= 600  ""1234668
           READ TABLE disqty INDEX 1.
         IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
           disqty-quantity = quan_to_dis.
            MODIFY disqty INDEX 1.
        ENDIF.
ENDIF. "If xkondh-chmvs >= 600

entry.title: Re: Purchasing organization for direct and indirect procurement processes.
entry.content:
If your concern is reporting then you can always find some workaround. It depends on your direct / indirect definition and it can vary. You can look at material valuation, material number presence, material group, purchasing group etc. Purchasing organization might not be the key here.

However, the situation is different in the case of authorisation, and I would advise you to contact a security expert who understands the concepts of authorisation in SAP. These concepts are technical, and from my experience I can tell, they are not intuitive and cause a lot of misunderstandings. You can start from reading those notes:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2846590

https://me.sap.com/notes/2203200

https://me.sap.com/notes/2996981

Purchasing organization might be the key element in your authorization concept, so it is not a straightforward decision on how to proceed.

entry.title: Re: What is the purpose of recognition and re-recognition...
entry.content: Thanks for your answer. Could you please help one more time by clarifying which of the following answers are true in the basis.I am a bit confued with 1) and 4). 1)To provide a training plan for authors to avoid authoring limitations 2)To find out if custom profiles need to be created 3)To find out what content types to produce for which target application 4) To set the customer expectations to a realistic level regarding recognition and re-recognition 5)To sell additional SAP Enable Now components

entry.content: very insightful

entry.title: How to Manage New Tariffs
entry.content:
Hello,

New tariffs have been announced in the USA. They go into effect this weekend.

What is the best practice for setting up import and export tariffs?

I have seen suggestions pointing to pricing procedures or using the SAP addon GTS.

entry.title: can someone help dpeloy python application(with openCV library included)on Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
so i developed a python application using paddleocr and when tried to deploy on Cloud Foundry, facing an issue with the error "ImportError libGL.so.1 cannot open shared object file No such file or directory" . Looked-up in the internet and found few threads that when used "open-python-headless" in requirements.txt file would resolve it but that didn't work.


Any idea how to fix this??

entry.title: Intercompany Billing without Sales Order or STO
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I'd like to receive some suggestion about intercompany billing in this scenario that I am following:

- we are using PS to manage customer projects: several WBS elements are used to generate material requirements directly in 1 or more manufacturing units, using network activities for each manufacturing plant; materials are produced in manufacturing plants and then delivered (with CNS0) to the installation site. no Purchase Order or STO but requirements directly in the manufacturing unit from where is delivered to installation site.

- a sales order exist for the entire project and will create customer billing based on billing plan.

- Resource related intercompany billing will be used for intercompany billing for hours registered for installation activities 

- Legal and group valuation exist

how would you suggest to trigger intercompany billing?

entry.title: How to setup Uninterrupted Sequential Numbering for MIRO Docs per Company in S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are implementing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for our company, an FCMG distributor operating in Slovakia, Czechia, Poland, and Hungary. Our setup includes one HQ (Slovakia) and three daughter companies, which exclusively purchase goods from HQ via Advanced Intercompany Sales (5D2).

We are currently configuring accounting document numbering for accounting documents in MIRO, and our Accounting department raised a critical legal requirement:

Local tax authorities in our countries require uninterrupted, sequential numbering per company code for audit and compliance reasons. If document numbers are not continuous per company, tax authorities could assume that invoices are being hidden or deleted.

We have been advised by our SAP partner that S/4HANA Public Cloud does not support company-specific document number ranges. Instead, document numbers are assigned sequentially across all company codes within the same document type.

Are there any best practices or configurations that allow uninterrupted numbering per company?

Any insights or experiences would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: S4 Public Cloud - EWM - How to print bin label
entry.content:
Hi emmanuelm1

I believe this is not supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to print the bin label.

So you would need to do this outside the system.

As you noticed, you can get a list of the bins in the warehouse monitor and export to a spreadsheet. 

I have done this in the past, then used a barcode font, for example, to create barcodes on the sheet and then print them on formatted labels.

Alternatively, you can pull the bin information from the system using the public API https://api.sap.com/api/WAREHOUSESTORAGEBIN_0001/overview

You could query all the information you need (you can even make an odata call from Excel for example to pull the info you need) and then print the label.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Content Transport Using SAP Cloud Platform Transport Management Service in SAP CPI CF Environme
entry.content:
Hi ,

 trial supports only one sub account for integration suite subscription, we can not create two sub accounts(for DEV and QA) in one tria account.i will have two trial accounts and two sub accounts(1st sub account  for Dev and 2nd subaccount for QA).

Can we implement Cloud Transport management between two sub accounts of different trails accounts?, Can you please  let me know.

 

Regards,

Narendar

Hi ,

Can we implement Cloud Transport management between two sub accounts of different trails accounts?, Can you please  let me know.

Narendar

entry.title: Re: convert a fixed term employed employee to a permanent...
entry.content: No these two fields don't have an connection.

entry.title: Re: SFTP using Rise with SAP( no PI )
entry.content:
aatan,

You will need to verify if the SAP RISE (ECS) team will allow this to be activated - check with your TSM/CDM

The solution we use is fairly easy -

1) Created an AWS FSX server - which has a shared folder (windows and linux)

2) Ask SAP ECS to mount the shared folder from the shared folder

3) Use an external tool to execute the SFTP commands

James

entry.title: ALM Summit Philadelphia PA
SDT Using SAP Business Transformation Center: End to End Process & Technical Configuration
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP Business Transformation Center is a SaaS (software-as-a-service) product from SAP, embedded into SAP Cloud ALM. It is a one-stop shop for customers who want to selectively transition from SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ERP) 6.08 to SAP S/4HANA 2023 or beyond using through SHELL CONVERSION method of SDT. Their target can be either to S/4 HANA on-premises or SAP S/4 HANA private cloud edition under RISE. In general, it is available to all the customer with a valid SAP Service Cloud subscriptions and the SAP Enterprise Support agreement and does not involve any additional license cost for usage.

In this blog i will show you End-to-End migration process and setup the connectivity between source and target system with SAP Cloud ALM.

Technical System Landscape:

SAP Business Transformation Center is a cloud native application which relies on the following components:

  • The source system, which utilizes the current ST-PI add-on to enable the data transformation with SAP Business Transformation Center.
  • The SAP S/4HANA system, which contains all necessary subcomponents already shipped within the SAP S/4HANA CORE component.

Each system is accessed from the cloud layer using the SAP Cloud Connector. The SAP ECC system and the SAP S/4HANA system are connected via OData service bindings from SAP Business Technology Platform. There is an RFC-based connection between the SAP ECC and the SAP S/4HANA system.

Source system:
ECC 6.0 EHP 8
OS: SuSE Linux 15.2
DB: SAP ASE 16.0.04.01

Target system:
Release: SAP S4HANA 2023 FPS02
OS: SuSE Linux 15.5
DB: SAP HANA DB 2.00.081.00.1733303410

Target system preparation for BTC:

In a shell conversion scenario, all data from the source SAP ERP system is removed, leaving the system with the customizing data only (Without master and transaction data). This empty shell system is then converted to SAP S/4HANA using SUM DMO. After the completion of the conversion, the selected master and transaction data is then migrated to the newly converted S/4HANA system.

Prerequisites Technical Aspects
1. Execution of SAP Readiness Check for conversion
2. Execution of SAP Readiness Check for Usage and Data Profiling
3. Create target system:
a) Creation of a Shell based on customer ECC
b) Conversion of ECC Shell to SAP S/4HANA with SAP SUM and provide as target system

Prerequisites Functional Aspects:
A. Following SAP S/4HANA conversion guide (link):
a) Simplification items
b) CVI
c) Custom Code adaption
d) Inconsistencies

End-to-End Migration Process and activities in SAP BTC:

Technical configuration and connectivity of source and target system:

1. Onboarding a New ABAP Source System
a. Technical Prerequisites and Required Add-Ons
b. Required SAP Notes
c. OData Services for Source System
d. Cloud Connector for Source System
e. Connect a Source ABAP System to SAP Cloud ALM
f. Maintenance of Property Store in the Connected Source Systems
g. Additional Technical Prerequisites for Source System
h. Next Steps After Onboarding a Source System

Technical Prerequisites and Required Add-Ons:

Minimum software component version requirement

Our current version

SAP_APPL 618

SAP_APPL 618 SP15

ST-PI 740 SP>=24

ST-PI 740 SP27

DMIS 2011_1_731 >=SP18

DMIS 2011_1_731 SP26


Required SAP Notes:

3443741 - Collective note for SAP Business Transformation Center
Implement following S-Notes: 3548580

OData Services for Source System:

Activate below O-Data Services in source system-
T-Code: /N/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE
/BDTS/COMMAND_SRV
/BDTS/INSTALLED_SAP_NOTE_SRV
/BDTS/PRESEL_TAB_DATA_SRV
/BDTS/PRESEL_TAB_READ_NAME_SRV
/BDTS/PRESEL_TASK_IS_CREATED_SRV
/BDTS/PRESEL_TASK_PORTION_SRV
/BDTS/PRESEL_TASK_READ_STATUS_SRV
/BDTS/SOFTWARE_COMPONENT_SRV
/BDTS/TABLE_FIELD_STRUCTUR_SRV

Cloud Connector for Source System:

1. Install Cloud Connector
2. Connect your SAP Cloud ALM subaccount to the cloud connector
3. Add backend source system in the cloud connector with HTTPS protocol

Connect a Source ABAP System to SAP Cloud ALM:

  • Connect the ABAP system to cloud ALM using the 'Landscape management'
  • T-Code /SDF/ALM_SETUP
  • Add endpoint for Business transformation center in the connected system and test the connectivity


Maintenance of Property Store in the Connected Source Systems:

  • Execute the /BDTS/ADMIN transaction.
  • Double-click on the /BDTS/PROPERTIES_SET object, or select this object and click Execute.
  • In the Property Store Maintenance transaction, you can create, change or delete a property.

Next Steps After Onboarding a Source System:

  • Generate Analysis File:
  • To generate an analysis file in the SAP ECC System, follow the instructions from SAP Note 3275056
  • For analysis file implement below two s-note
  • 3112362 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling
  • 3275056 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling: Initial Data Migration Profile 2.0
  • Execute the program RC_UDP_START_DMR2 via transaction SA38 in the productive client of the production system to start the analysis
  • Choose Schedule Analysis to schedule the background job to collect the data.
  • Once the data collection job is finished, choose the Download DMR2 Analysis Data option from within the program RC_UDP_START_DMR2  to download the analysis data to your local file system.
  • Upload the analysis file in Cloud ALM

Run system scan in Cloud ALM:

Like we had scoped the company code and transformation object for SDT, we must also check the relevance of each of the Z object and scope it based on the customer requirement or based on its last Activity after discussing with the object owner. This scoping can be performed using 'Select Scanned Table' app as shown below. Please note that any Z table with record count zero is automatically marked as out of scope by SAP BTC.

Onboarding of target S/4 HANA Shell to SAP BTC:

Technical Prerequisites and Required Add-Ons
Required SAP Notes
OData Services for Target System
Cloud Connector for Target System
Connect a Target ABAP System to SAP Cloud ALM
Create RFC Destination to SAP ECC Source System
Maintenance of Property Store in the Connected Target System
Save the Upgrade Buffers
Start the Command Daemon
Next Steps After Onboarding a Target System

Technical Prerequisites and Required Add-Ons:

Ensure that the minimum version requirement
S4COREOP 108, with corresponding support package level >=FPS1.
ST-PI >=740, with corresponding support package level >=0024.

Required SAP Notes:

Ensure to apply the SAP note 3443741 in target system.

OData Services for Target System:

In the SAP S/4HANA target system, some components from the ST-PI add-on are reused, therefore both OData V2 and V4 services need to be activated.

OData V2 services:
T-Code: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICES
/BDTS/INSTALLED_SAP_NOTE_SRV
/BDTS/SOFTWARE_COMPONENT_SRV
/BDTS/TABLE_FIELD_STRUCTUR_SRV

OData V4 services:
T-Code: /IWFND/V4_ADMIN
API_BDTSCOMMAND
API_BDTSDESIGNTIMEENTITY
API_BDTSEXECUTIONPLAN
API_BDTSHEALTHCHECK
API_BDTSMIGRATIONSTATUS
API_BDTSTABLEFIELDSTRUCT
API_BDTSVALUEMAPPINGDATA

Cloud Connector for Target System:

1. Install Cloud Connector
2. Connect your SAP Cloud ALM subaccount to the cloud connector
3. Add backend target system in the cloud connector with HTTPS protocol

Connect a Target ABAP System to SAP Cloud ALM:

  • Connect the ABAP system to cloud ALM using the 'Landscape management'
  • T-Code /SDF/ALM_SETUP
  • Add endpoint for Business transformation center in the connected system and test the connectivity

Create RFC Destination to SAP ECC Source System:

Create RFC Destination to SAP ECC Source System in target system and maintain the property store in target as described in Maintenance of Property Store in SAP S/4HANA System.

This RFC will be used by the migration engine of SAP BTC to pull data packages from the SAP ECC source system and hence must be maintained in the property RfcDestinationSender in t-code bdts_admin-> Select BDTS_PROPERTIES_SET.

Maintenance of Property Store in the Connected Target System:

Save the Upgrade Buffers:

During the shell conversion process, the upgrade buffer of the system was updated and this must be saved

  1.   Execute the BDTS_ADMIN transaction.
  2.   Double-click on BDTS_DMC_SAVE_UPG_TRANS object or select Execute.
  3.   Select Save transports from upgrade

Start the Command Daemon:

To enable the handling of cloud commands on the ABAP side, the Command Daemon must be activated

  1. Execute the BDTS_ADMIN transaction.
  2. Double-click on BDTS_CMD_WDOG_START object or select it and click Execute.
  3. Enter BDTS_T_DAE as the daemon user (or whatever you set up for this task) and set the frequency. The recommended frequency to use is 1.000 milliseconds.
  4. In case the daemon is already running, a corresponding notification is issued.

Next Steps After Onboarding a Target System:

Now that your target system is onboarded, you can run a final check from the BDTS_ADMIN transaction with the BDTS_ADM_SETUP_CHECK health check, as described in Health Checks. This setup check runs health checks that are specific for the setup.

In this way we can prepare and stablished the connectivity for source and target system with SAP Cloud ALM.

Next steps after the technical configuration-

Create digital blueprint
Create transformation model and version
Create cycle and Run cycle

Once we create a version of transformation model from the blueprint and connect it to the source ECC system and target S/4 HANA system, it triggers the application data migration from source to the target.  It happens in 4 phases as shown below.

PHASE 1: CYCLE PREVALIDATION (UPTIME)
PHASE 2: CYCLE PREPARATION (UPTIME)
PHASE 3: RUN CYCLES (DOWNTIME)
PHASE 4: POSTPROCESS CYCLE (DOWNTIME)

All these phases can be managed using the "Manage Cycle" app in SAP BTC.

1.Cycle Prevalidation:

During the "Cycle prevalidation" phase, several prerequisites of the connected systems are examined.  Making sure there is no business data on the target S/4 HANA shell system. Any problems found are recorded in the cycle log as indicated, and they can be carried out repeatedly until this phase turns green.

2. Cycle Preparation:

In 'Cycle preparation' phase, the actual preparation and configuration of the migration engine takes place. This phase runs as a part of uptime phase and runs before the cutover. This step along with initial step up, also covers transformation rule creation, sub-transformation object creation and validation followed by the postprocessing items creation. 

3. Run Cycles:

Based on the specified and scoped elements in the transformation model, the third phase, "Run Cycles," involves the actual physical data migration to the target S/4 SHELL.

4. Postprocess Cycles:

In the final step of "Post-processing", all the data that was transferred directly at database table level gets converted into SAP S/4 HANA model. Like all phases, logging is available which enables us to view the progress at a granular level.

This blog covers End to End Process & Technical Configuration for SDT using SAP BTC.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: Re: Error "403 Forbidden The request has been blocked by...
entry.content:
Hi RodoFK

Could you please provide some more details about how you are calling the service?

Calling the GET does rely on the proper Authentication, but calling a POST or PATCH requires some extra validations.

For example, are you filling the request header "x-csrf-token" with "fetch" on the GET and then pass that received value into the POST "x-csrf-token"? You may also have to pay attention to etag (i.e. IF-None-Match header) - but maybe not with POST.

Typically Client errors such as 401 (Unauthorized – usually a session or authentication problem) or 403 (Forbidden – usually an invalid token issue) - so I suspect it is the cross-site scripting protection (x-csrf-token).

Please check this blog

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/s-4hana-cloud-x-csrf-token-and-e-tag-validation/ba-p/13427700

 

Also please be aware to properly manage your sessions 

note 2760552 - Limitation of generation of HTTP security sessions)

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP function and action with Unstructured/Dynamic Type Input/Output
entry.content:
Hi @Ajit_K_Panda 

i am trying to use the same as our requirement is to get json payload (having 1800 properties)from solace and consume this payload and call the backend service.

But some how this is not working for me. Could this be a node version?

Regards,

Vikash

entry.title: Setting freight order items to loaded with API
entry.content:
We are building custom made fiori applications for transportation management to be used with mobile devices. As a part of that, we would need execute with API call functionality "Set Items to Loaded" that is done done in application "Load or Unload Freight Orders" when processing freight orders in S4. More details are provided in attachment. We have managed to do with APIs all the other steps in outbound delivery order and freight order processing but this step is still missing. 

Has someone done this kind of development? Please provide ideas and examples how to do this.

entry.title: SAP Order-to-Cash (OTC) Process – Detailed Explanation with Real-Time Example
entry.content:
Hello All,

In our internal team discussions, recently we have had many points on OTC process, wanted to summarise them here for further discussion.

Order-to-Cash (OTC) is a crucial process stream that integrates with other key streams such as Purchase-to-Pay (PTP) and Material Management (MM), and inventory, covering both master and transactional data.

This process acts as a bridge between customers, vendors, stock, and inventory, ensuring seamless transactions and efficient business operations.

Let's discuss the detailed steps here:

 OTC Process Steps:

  1. Order Creation (Sales Order Entry)

    • A customer submits a purchase order (PO) or requests a quote.

    • The sales representative creates a Sales Order (SO) in SAP.

    • Customer credit checks are performed.

  2. Order Processing (Sales Order Confirmation & Approval)

    • The system verifies the order details (pricing, quantity, discounts, taxes).

    • Sales Order is confirmed and approved for further processing.

  3. Inventory Check & Fulfillment (Material Availability & Delivery Processing)

    • The system checks stock availability or initiates procurement (if out of stock).

    • A delivery document is created for the warehouse team to process.

  4. Shipping & Delivery (Goods Issue & Tracking)

    • Goods are picked, packed, and shipped (for physical products).

    • For software licenses, digital keys or login credentials are generated and shared.

    • The system updates delivery status and tracking details are sent to the customer.

  5. Invoicing (Billing Document Generation)

    • A billing document (invoice) is generated in SAP and sent to the customer.

    • Tax calculations and compliance checks are done automatically.

  6. Payment Collection (Accounts Receivable Management)

    • The customer makes the payment via bank transfer, credit card, or online payment gateway.

    • The payment is recorded in the system, and accounts receivable is updated.

  7. Accounting & Reporting (Revenue Recognition & Financial Reporting)

    • The finance team reconciles payments and records revenue in SAP.

    • Reports are generated for revenue forecasting and audits.

Real-Time Example: Venkatesh Golla Software Solutions – SAP License Sales

Scenario: Selling 50 SAP BODS Licenses to AB Corp

  1. Order Creation

    • AB Corp requests 50 SAP BODS licenses.

    • The sales team enters the order in SAP with price, quantity, and terms.

    • Customer credit check is performed.

  2. Order Processing

    • The order is reviewed and approved.

    • System validates pricing and discounts.

  3. Inventory Check & Fulfillment

    • SAP system verifies available license stock.

    • If needed, new licenses are procured from SAP.

  4. Shipping & Delivery

    • The system generates license keys and activation instructions.

    • The digital licenses are sent via email with tracking confirmation.

  5. Invoicing

    • An invoice is generated in SAP.

    • The invoice is emailed to AB Corp's finance department.

  6. Payment Collection

    • AB Corp makes an NEFT transfer of ₹50,00,000 (example).

    • The finance team records the transaction.

  7. Accounting & Reporting

    • Revenue is recorded, and financial reports are updated.

    • A report is generated for management to track sales performance.

Why This Process Matters?

✅ Ensures smooth sales operations.
✅ Helps track payments and revenue.
✅ Reduces errors with automated workflows.
✅ Enhances customer experience with seamless order processing.

Let's continue the series and discuss further.

Thanks,

Venkatesh Golla

entry.title: Configure and understand the forecasting in the Indicator Monitoring tab in a Technical Object.
entry.content:
1. How do I configure an indicator to be to use the forecast functionality in Indicator Monitoring?
  2. How do I display the forecast in Indicator Monitoring?
  3. How does the Indicator Monitoring chart calculates the data points?
  4. How do I interpret the forecast chart?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Configure and understand the forecasting in the Indic...
entry.content:
1. How do I configure an indicator to be used in the forecast functionality in Indicator Monitoring?
    1. Open a technical object.
    2. Select the Indicators tab.
    3. Select an indicator that has values.
    4. Select the Configure button.
    5. Select the Forecast tab within the Configure popup window.
    6. Enable the forecast (left hand side) and chose the past data range (right hand side).
    7. Select Save.
  2. How do I display the forecast in Indicator Monitoring?
    1. Following on from above.
    2. Select the Indicator Monitoring tab.
    3. Select the Duration wished to be displayed.
    4. Select the Select Indicators button and select which indicator(s) to be displayed.
    5. Select the Settings button (the cog near Select Indicators).
    6. Enable the Forecast and set the duration of it and select Apply.
    7. The forecast chart will then be calculated and displayed beside the duration chart.

  3. How does the Indicator Monitoring chart calculates the data points?
    The visualisation logic relies on grouping data points into time-based aggregates to improve performance and simplify the display. So differences or missing data maybe noticed for the last 6 hours or longer time windows as opposed to 4 hours:
    1. Aggregation Logic:
      1. The smallest time unit for aggregation is 2 minutes. This means that, for every hour, there are 30 aggregates (since 60 minutes divided by 2 equals 30).
      2. When viewing a chart with a 4-hour window, the system needs to divide the data into 125 divisions. However, since 4 hours only provides 120 aggregates (4 x 30 = 120), the system uses raw data to calculate the visualisation, which can produce different results.
      3. For a 6-hour window, there are 180 aggregates available (6 x 30 = 180), which is more than enough to fill the 125 divisions. In this case, the system uses the pre-computed 2-minute aggregates, which can cause differences in the displayed values compared to the 4-hour view.
    2. Lead Time for Aggregates calculation:
      1. There is a 15-minute lead time for calculating these pre-computed aggregates. So, if the chart relies on these aggregates, there is a chance that the most recent data points (within the last 15 minutes) might be missing. This explains why the last few data points are missing for the 6 hours duration.
      Recommendation to ensure you visualise all the recent data points:
    1. Zoom in to view the data for the last 4 hours duration or less. This will force the system to calculate the visualisation using raw data, ensuring all the data points are included.


  4. How do I interpret the forecast chart?
    The forecast will be more accurate if the Range of past data to be considered value used is a large range. So if a small range is chosen (1 day) for the indicator forecast, then the chart will show a larger variable range (larger cone) within the forecast chart. If the range on the indicator forecast is larger (1 month of example) then the chart will show a smaller variable range (small cone) within the forecast chart. The larger an indicator ranger (bigger the data set) the more accurate the forecast, this results in smaller variation of value ranges (smaller cone).

entry.title: Re: Hello, I have a dump in transaction MFBF
entry.content:
Hi David,

xkondh-chmvs contains 600, which results in PERFORM CHMVS_600 IN PROGRAM SAPLV01F.

this table: TCHMVS

look for entries with 600.

Thanks,

Arun

entry.title: Influence the Future of SAP Signavio Solutions – Your Experience. Your Voice. Our Next Chapter.
entry.content:
We know the best ideas often come from solving everyday challenges - navigating systems, solving issues, and making processes run smoothly. Whether you're a process owner, business analyst, enterprise architect, or simply someone who works with our solutions day to day, your hands-on experience gives you a unique position to suggest meaningful improvements.

Ever found yourself thinking, "If only this feature worked differently, my job would be so much simpler!" You're not alone — and that moment of insight is exactly what we want to hear.

That's why we invite you to visit the SAP Continous Influence Portal for SAP Signavio solutions — a space created for our SAP Signavio community where you can submit your ideas, share feedback, and help shape our portfolio with insights from your daily work.

This is not a commitment. It's an open invitation to bring your experience to the table and spark improvements that scale across organizations, industries, and use cases.

Your Challenges, Our Priority

Everyone brings something different to transformation—from cross-functional alignment to operational efficiency. Your ideas help us adapt and innovate with relevance; through the portal, you can submit improvement requests related to:

  • SAP Signavio Process Insights
  • SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
  • SAP Signavio Process Manager
  • SAP Signavio Journey Modeler
  • SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub
  • SAP Signavio Process Governance
  • SAP Signavio Transformation Suite
  • SAP Signavio Value Accelerators
  • SAP Signavio Process Explorer
  • Process AI for SAP Signavio solutions
  • Cross-product improvement requests

Whether you are looking for more intuitive dashboards, simplified modeling tools, or better integration—this is your space to be heard.

How to Contribute

Getting started is easy:

  1. Create an account or log in on the SAP Continuous Influence portal.
  2. Visit the SAP Continuous Influence portal for SAP Signavio solutions.
  3. Submit ideas, vote for suggestions, leave comments, and follow topics relevant to your work.

Improvement requests with five or more votes are reviewed during our scheduled review cycles. That means your feedback isn't just collected—it's considered and acted upon.

Let's Build the Future Together 

The best product decisions come from the people using our products every day. By sharing your ideas, you help prioritize what's most relevant — not just for your team but for the entire SAP Signavio community. 

So, if you've ever paused mid-task and thought, "This could be better," now is the time to act. Your experience might be the insight that drives the next wave of innovation.

👉Ready to shape the future of business process management?

Visit the SAP Continuous Influence portal for SAP Signavio solutions to get started.

entry.title: ALM Summit Philadelphia PA
entry.content:
On March 23-26, SAP hosted an ALM Summit in Philadelphia, PA for customers, business partners and SAP employees at the beautiful Newtown Square Offices.  A colleague and I had the opportunity to attend.  The three-day conference had a great turnout -- there were 207 people registered for the event.  (We were told that this was a new record and the first conference of its kind since 2022.)  Our business partners sponsored the event food and nightly activities. (I extend a big 'Thank You!' to our partners for this sponsorship.)

During the different days of the conference, there were multiple tracks people could choose to attend.  The tracks accommodated the various attendee levels:  new customers who have not yet begun their Cloud ALM conversion, current users who are using portions of Cloud ALM (mainly Operations) and others who are fully adopting the service.  The first day had mainly sessions where everyone came together, but day 2 broke out into three tracks: Operations, Implementation and Service sessions while day 3 broke out again into three tracks:  Operations and Implementation as well as a third option of cross track sessions.

Various sessions dealing with “where to start“ on Cloud ALM, availability of fully equipped Operations use cases (move now!), the current and future state of Implementation use cases, Clean Core initiative to ease upgrade processes, explanations of SAP Best Practice/SAP Activate Methodology integration to take advantage of tasks/test cases/etc compiled by SAP which save them substantial effort in implementing/utilizing Cloud ALM functionality, Readiness Check report functionality, the Cloud ALM Toolchain (Signavio, Tricentis, WalkMe, LeanIX), Selective Data Transfer option to move their SOLDOC elements, and so much more.  Sessions also showcased some of the AI initiatives we have ongoing.  We were shown a demo on how development intends to integrate Joule into our Operations use cases in Alerting -> one more way to help customers solve their own problem right in the app without needing to engage Technical Support.

Throughout the three days, each sponsoring partner had a dedicated session where all attendees came together in the main SAP auditorium to listen about their solutions.  Of course, there was Q/A at the end of each session for people to ask their questions, but in the case time ran out, each partner had a booth outside the sessions for the duration of the conference.  They could showcase their solutions and allow customers to engage in asking further questions/information gathering.  SAP staff also manned a booth where customers could seek out information directly with SAP staff.

On the mornings of Tuesday and Wednesday, there were multiple 'Ask the expert' sessions.  This is where we came in during the Selective Data Transfer (SDT) session.  Attendees asked us, and others, questions about SDT.

Monday and Tuesday ended with a gathering for all attendees. Monday night, we remained at the Newtown Square facility and enjoyed some finger foods (sliders and appetizers) along with the option of a few beverages.  Tuesday night was a little fancier.  It was held off-site, closer to the hotels making it convenient to walk ‘home’ afterwards.  Drinks and appetizers were served while a band played cover songs (they were quite good) and all had further opportunity to meet and build relationships/network.  Dinner was later served while the band continued to perform for us.  The menu consisted of 3 stations: Southwest, Italian and ‘American’ (beef/salmon/potatoes).  Later, dessert was offered, but this attendee ducked out prior to its serving (I, for one, needed no more food!).

Probably my favorite part of the Summit was the final session of Monday and Tuesday where SAP staff came together on-stage for “Ask me anything” type of questioning.  The sessions were very engaging and ‘presenters’ were very professional, informed and genuine in answering anything and everything customers threw at them.  I was truly impressed by how much they all care about our Cloud ALM customer base and their success.

If you have the chance to attend an ALM Summit, I would certainly advise you take it.

entry.title: Re: Alumni Experience- 1H2025 Release
entry.content:

I get that for access to payslip or other pay-related forms, but is it really a prerequisite for the alumni feature as a whole? Even without EC it should still be able to give them access the EC data.

entry.title: Payment terms due date issue
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I had issue with due date

Invoice Date: April 4, 2025,

Posting Date: April 4, 2025

Payment Terms: 2% discount,

Fixed day 31,

Additional month -1

As per the above parameters due date should be May 31, but we are getting April 4  . Can some help me what was wrong.

entry.title: Re: Enable Now error upload of potentially dangerous cont...
entry.content: If that doesn't work, there is also a newer Permission in Manager under Administration > Permissions. Select the author role to modify and make sure Content: Upload Active Content is selected. If it's not, that role cannot save "active content" to the Manager.

entry.title: Re: Decimal overflow while calculating key figures
entry.content:
Hi,

Its quite late reply, this might still be helpful for others facing the same issue.

This error typically occurs due to an exception in the report RKKBCAL2. During the Analysis step in CK40N, the system reads all cost components at default costing lot size. If a particular cost component has an excessively large value, the system may encounter an overflow exception while converting the currency (Form `convert_curr_to_internal`). This happens when the total number of digits exceeds 13. When overflow occurs system change the value of variable to '9999999'.

To resolve this issue, please review the BOM for the affected materials.

Regards,

entry.title: Stop dunning procedures for correspondence dunning
entry.content:
Dear community,

I am using the incoming correspondence dunning functionalities of FS-CD. It works so far that I create an (expected) incoming correspondence. Once it is behind the due date and I run the transaction Correspondence Dunning (FPCODU) the dunning activities are executed. When the incoming document now gets an "received at" date I was expecting that a run of the dunning end run would stop the dunning procedure. This is not the case. Can somebody tell me what is necessary to stop/cancel the running dunning procedure?

Thanks a lot

entry.title: Re: standard smartforms
entry.content: Hello, we are currently preparing to enter the EU Zone for Bulgaria and I am curious, how can we edit the Smartforms for our Invoices? Our systems have been set up by an ERP Advisory, so this is not something that we have done for ourselves.

entry.title: Re: MB_MIGO_BADI - Facing "You are not allowed to change...
entry.content:
Hello @Sabeer_Sakkariya 

I'd suggest to use the BAdI as it is meant to be used, i.e. to enhance MIGO with custom fields displayed in dedicated custom tabs. I don't think modifying screen 200 is a good idea.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Not able to call a s/4hana(on premise) api using action projects from sap build process automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are trying to call s/4hana standard api for business partner using action projects.

Below is the partial url -

"sap/opu/odata/sap/md_businesspartner_srv/C_BPEmailAddress" that is setup in the destination.

While executing our automation code, it fails with  below errors-

error messages-

Action execution resulted in ECONNRESET with message read ECONNRESET

query ENOFOUND <<our hostname>>

note- our desktop agent version - 3.39.45

We have setup cloud connector setting as per guide and destination is also working fine.

The url works fine at browser level as well.

Has anybody noticed any similar errors recently?

This setup was working fine before but has now stopped working suddenly from jan 2025 onwards.

Please advise.

Regards,

Saritha

entry.title: Re: Problem with consumption in POD
entry.content:
Despite the very little information provided, it seems you are trying to complete this SFC in the last operation of the routing, which automatically posts goods receipt back to ERP, not to consume materials as the title suggest, and it is failing because the order header material is batch managed but a planned batch is missing in the order header.

If that´s the case, during order creation in ERP, you must make sure that a Planned Batch is created in Goods Receipt tab:

Which should be reflected in DM on Manage Orders app header:

Hope it helps,

Manoel

entry.title: CB User ID
entry.content:
We would like to create an identical CB User ID in both the Test and Production S/HANA Cloud Systems. Is this possible?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CB User ID
entry.content:
Please refer below document for your query:

CBUSER ID Discrepancy Between Test and Production Tenants

The CB User ID is the unique identifier for a business user or a technical user.

The system creates the user ID automatically and it is assigned during the user creation and will not be same in test and production system or any two systems.

You can also refer the Help Centre Document :

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/46d275f9b2ca47b8b199b020ea3f8bcf.html?q=Manage+workforce

Thanks

Akshara

entry.title: Modify SMARTFORM for Billing V3 - LB_BIL_INVOICE, SAP ECC
entry.content:
Good day,


My country is entering in the EU Zone, and I need to be start issuing invoices both in EURO and BGN as of June 2025. Can you please help me understand how I can achieve this? I have reached from NACE -> V3 -> Processing Routines and I see the smartform, but I am not sure how I can modify it for RD00 Invoices.


Thank you,
Nikola

entry.title: Allow addition to existing stock for products but prevent nested HUs
entry.content:
Hello,

what would be the easiest way to allow addition to existing HUs for Products but prevent the nesting of HUs? I tried with different settings on Storage-Type Level but it did not work. Grateful for any ideas.

Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: IBP Automated outlier correction
entry.content:
Hello @Sam119 


Did you check if there are any values for "Outlier Markers"? This is a parameter that you can set up in the system to define whether a time period should be or not considered an outlier.

Automatic Outlier Correction | SAP Help Portal


Best regards,
Vinicius Moura

entry.title: Virtual Info Session :Unlock Innovation with Design Thinking
entry.content:
Curious about innovation techniques? Whether you're an entrepreneur, business leader, or in academia — this is your invitation to discover one of the world’s most powerful innovation tools: Design Thinking.

Join us for a dynamic and interactive briefing session on April 7 to learn how you can receive top-tier training and certification — and bring transformative thinking to your team, company, or startup.

Register now!- https://www.eventbrite.de/e/impact-week-armenia-briefing-session-tickets-1308120125509?aff=oddtdtcreator 

When: April 7, 4pm CET

💡 Why Design Thinking?
Used by global giants like SAP, Google, Apple, and VW, this methodology turns bold ideas into real-world impact.

🎙️ Featuring:
Jochen Gürtler – One of Germany’s top Design Thinking experts
Former Innovation Manager at SAP and lecturer at Hasso Plattner Institute
Clients: Nestlé, Adidas, Daimler, and more

Masoumeh Moghadam – development project expert and Design Thinking coach at SAP

Carolin Taenzer, Managing Director of Impact Week

entry.title: Re: Automatically add account in involved parties to lead...
entry.content: My expected requirement is - he Account to be automatically added to the Involved Parties without selecting a Contact

entry.title: Contractual Working Time
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We have a problem regarding the display and calculation of "Contractual Working Time".

The Situation is as follows:

Customer uses Time Tracking in combination with a third party shift planning tool.
This tool imports individual workschedules for employees as per shift requirements.

However this lead to a situation where the schedule working time from the workschedule can in fact be different from the contractual working time as from the contract agreement.

This causes a miscalculation for FLEX times as the threshold is not correct.

 

Now to portrait and calculate that correctly in the system we implemented an "extra" time type called "Contractual Working Time" and put it via an aggregation TV into the time sheet.

We then replaced the scheduled working time in the FLEX TV with the extra time and it worked perfectly.

Then we made an SF to SF integration so that this process is performed daily and automatically.

This also works nicely HOWEVER there is just one catch:

Holidays need to be exempted from that integration. That might sound easy but is not easy to pull off at all.

I was wondering how to other companies do that? Because every company that has thirdparty shiftplanning tools as well has the same problem.

I already did a customer influence request which you can find here:
Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

Please share your ideas, comments and suggestions.


It is highly appreciated!

Thank you very much

Kind Regards,
Jan

entry.title: How do i create GL accounts code for a list of prepaid cards ( Assets )
entry.content:
SAP S4/HANA CLOUD FIORI

How to you create GL Accounts code for prepaid cards (debit card) - Assets?

What about also credit cards?

Do you treat it the same as banks?

entry.title: input tax distribution and correction according to § 15 of the German Value Added Tax Act (UStG)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

this is about the input tax distribution according to § 15 of the German Value Added Tax Act (UStG) and the input tax correction according to § 15a of the German Value Added Tax Act (UStG) in the SAP S4HANA Private Cloud.

 

Input tax distribution according to § 15 UStG or § 15 (4) UStG:

Input tax distribution means dividing the input tax amounts into two parts, one part, the non-deductible input tax amount, and the other part, the deductible input tax amount.

The amounts of the input tax which are deductible and not, is determined by their allocation to the revenues. If the input tax is allocated to the revenues subject to VAT, the input tax is deductible; if the input tax is allocated to the revenues exempt from VAT, the input tax is not deductible.

 

For this allocation, an allocation key is calculated as follows: VAT-exempt revenues are divided by VAT-liable revenues, resulting in the allocation key.

The input tax amounts are then divided into deductible input tax and non-deductible input tax using this allocation key, and posted in the system.

 

Input tax correction according to § 15a of the German Value Added Tax Act (UStG):

At the end of the fiscal year, after all revenues have been posted, the allocation key is recalculated and updated.

The input tax correction is executed using the updated key. All posted input tax amounts which were calculated using the old allocation key, are corrected by using the new allocation key.

 

This needs to be done in the accounts receivable ledger, the general ledger, and for assets in the fixed asset ledger.

 

What options does SAP S4HANA Private Cloud offer for the accounts receivable subledger, the general ledger, and the fixed asset subledger?

Is there a way to use a Tax on Sales/Purchases code for this, which can then be updated and which corrects the input tax amounts automatically?

 

I would be very thankful for any answer or idea, would be highly appreciated. 🙂

 

Best regards,

A. Regensburger

entry.title: Re: Enter Asset Transaction: Post Depreciation Manually -...

entry.title: Re: Enter Asset Transaction: Post Depreciation Manually -...
entry.content: Hi, Jeremy! I tried the solution and it works! Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Deletion of CI_COBL
entry.content:
So you didn't read 3456968 - Deletion of coding block fields from CI_COBL ...

'The only recommendation is to customize the field as suppressed in all field status groups (in transaction OB14 and OB41) to hide the field from entry or display in any standard transaction, but you should NEVER delete the field from CI_COBL structure or any database table it was included.'

entry.title: Inserted actions do not work anymore
entry.content:
Hello 

We are facing an issue with recordings in Sap Enable Now since this week. 

When you look at a demo, the clicks that are added after generating the simulation are in the left upper corner of the screen and not in the place we put them. It seems to be that it's only happening to the manually added clicks and not in the ones that are generated in the process of the recording. But before, it worked just fine for the added actions/clicks. 

Is this a known issue? Can we do something about it? 

Kind regards 

Laura

entry.title: ABAP DUMP 'SAPLV13A' DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR. How to handle this DUMP in SAP
entry.content: This happens when condition for which we are loading this per 0 scale value. The purpose is so that when the quantity of the material group on the order reaches 5000 plus they no longer pay the freight surcharge rate.

entry.title: Absence Time Type Writes 0 Hours on Holidays
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm trying to create a custom absence time type (e.g., Holiday Substitute) in SAP SuccessFactors Time Off, intended only for reporting purposes — specifically, to record 8 hours on public holidays and non-working days even though the day has no scheduled hours (holidays show 0 in Planned Working Time).
My goals are to add a time type to appear in Story Reports and Time Sheet UI and that time type must write a fixed 8-hour duration even on non-working days or holidays (No payroll integration involved – this is purely for reporting.

The method I used: 

1. Absence Counting Configuration with seemingly correct configuration and business rule assigned:  
IF <conditions> (always true)
THEN Absence Counting Rule Result Parameters.Deduction Quantity = 8

2. Defined Time Type Collision Grouping - The collision does resolve correctly when overlapping absences are created — the new time type replaces the previous one.

Result is in the Time Sheet, when this time type is used on a holiday, it shows up but with 0 hours and the same duration is written in Recorded time data v2 query table. 

I also tried creating time valuations with valuation type:  1. calculation by business rule and 2. create time type but these require Time Tracking to be enabled, which is not available in our license.

Is there a way to bypass the 0-hour issue when using only Time Off (no Time Tracking)? Is my rule maybe the issue?
Any workarounds, tips, or examples of similar setups would be greatly appreciated!
Thanks in advance!

entry.content:
Hello folks,

Thanks,

Vinay
